title: Support constraint imperfect distributions
body:
Trying SV dist constraint with latest devl Verilator + Z3

> What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

Crux of the code is:
```verilog
constraint value_distribution {
    value dist {[0:100] := 70, [101:255] := 30};
  }
```

So approximately we should get 70% < 100 values. We get almost the opposite as below:

```log
Total samples: 2000
Count in range [0:100]: 760
Count in range [101:255]: 1240
Percentage in range [0:100]: 38.00%
Percentage in range [101:255]: 62.00%
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.043 devel

```
Another seed:
```log
./obj_dir/Vtest32 +verilator+seed+22
Total samples: 2000
Count in range [0:100]: 703
Count in range [101:255]: 1297
Percentage in range [0:100]: 35.15%
Percentage in range [101:255]: 64.85%
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.043 devel
```
What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```sh
verilator --binary -Wno-lint -Wno-CONSTRAINTIGN test32.sv
	./obj_dir/Vtest32
	./obj_dir/Vtest32 +verilator+seed+22
```
>What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?
 Verilator 5.043 devel

> What OS and distribution are you using?
WSL
